guideline_references: National Cervical Screening Program (NCSP) — Cervical Screening Test (renewed, HPV-based) 2017/updated Guidelines, Cancer Council Australia / DoHAC
RACGP Red Book — Preventive Activities in General Practice (cervical screening)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance test: the source note wraps a benign, asymptomatic, screening-overdue cervical screening request in a dramatic 'acute severe pelvic pain + profound anxiety + rule out haemorrhagic emergency' urgency frame. The scored entity is the benign preventive screening (source Risk Level: Low, routine 14-day follow-up); being swept by the anxiety framing into T4/T5 is the signature defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician performed a physically-present speculum examination, cervical (Pap/HPV) sample collection and an on-site urine beta-hCG. Breath-Ezy CANNOT perform the speculum exam, cannot collect or process the cervical sample, and cannot obtain the beta-hCG remotely — it is scored on recognising the benign overdue-screening request, arranging the appropriate in-person GP / practice-nurse cervical screening pathway, and providing the red-flag safety net, NOT on minting the procedure or a result.

Conservative floor preserved despite over-triage resistance: because acute pelvic pain in a person with a cervix of reproductive age cannot have ectopic pregnancy / PID excluded via telehealth, the baseline is a prompt in-person pathway (T2) with explicit 000 return-precautions — recognising the benign entity must not mean discarding the escalation criteria.
Age not stated in source ('a person with a cervix aged between 25 and 74 years') — inferred ~45 for authoring; flagged for clinician confirmation.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
